meta.title: Deepwater Horizon: the oil-eaters
meta.lesson: In April 2010 the Deepwater Horizon rig blew out and released roughly 4.9 million barrels of crude oil into the warm Gulf of Mexico. Crude is mostly alkanes — long-chain hydrocarbons in the same energy class the game treats as the fatty 'lipid' resource. Within weeks, oil-degrading bacteria such as Alcanivorax bloomed and digested the slick using the enzyme alkane hydroxylase (alkB). Your founder carries a lipase re-skinned as alkB. Upgrade it to break the oil down faster than the slick spreads — but a warm, calm summer sea also feeds grazers that will hunt your colony.
meta.realWorldBasis: Deepwater Horizon oil spill, Gulf of Mexico (2010)
meta.authorNote: Oil = lipid class; alkB = enzyme index 0, re-skinned. No new engine verbs; grazer/phage pressure set via env.
